page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.large is a KVM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.c8a family. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 9T24 cores running at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The instance features 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is capped at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test shows strong performance, placing in the top 25% with a score of 3059.78. However, multi-core CPU performance is weak, scoring 3062.79 (bottom 25%), reflecting the limited 2-core configuration. Memory performance is also constrained; while large-block write bandwidth is average at 27.53 GB/sec, read bandwidth for small cached blocks is poor at 150.55 GB/sec (bottom 10%). Passmark metrics reflect these limitations, with a CPU score of 3799.5 and a database operations score of 1020.44, both falling in the lower percentiles.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and low resource footprint, the ecs.c8a.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-focused workloads that do not require large memory capacities or local storage. It is suitable for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving (achieving a static web serving throughput of 2.79 GB/sec), basic cryptography, and simple utility services. However, it is not recommended for memory-intensive databases, parallelized multi-core processing, or high-throughput data pipelines due to its poor database operations score and limited memory bandwidth.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor running at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM, 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU. The processor supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and AES. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, scoring 3059.78 in stress-ng tests, though multi-core and memory read bandwidth metrics remain in the lower percentiles. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, compute-bound workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU resources but require minimal memory. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and simple utility tasks rather than memory-intensive database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.large is a 2-vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM compute-optimized instance powered by AMD EPYC 9T24 processors with dedicated CPU allocation.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core compute instance featuring strong single-core performance and advanced instruction sets for lightweight, compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9T24 processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
4.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 compute-optimized ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Support for AVX-512 vector instructions and AES hardware cryptography
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded utility tasks
